user_priority: TV first if movies and TV cannot progress concurrently

retry_held.retry_policy: source_or_release_only; title remains wanted and retryable

tv_first_expansion.plex_visible_tv_publication_blocker: Private direct-stream artifacts must be bridged through materialized/WebDAV-safe TV path before Plex-visible surfacing.

mission002_iptv.blocker: Plex account Online Media Sources opt-out requires valid account token or authenticated Chrome extension session
mission002_iptv.plex_online_channels_limitation: This local suppression blocks the Plex free-live-TV provider endpoint on this Windows/Plex host. Plex's official durable fix is still account-level Online Media Sources disablement; other client devices may need account opt-out or local/network DNS enforcement if they fetch Plex online channels directly.

next_safe_actions: Build the TV materialized/WebDAV adapter or bridge for the 3 validated private-stage TV candidates.
Do not publish the private direct-stream .strm artifacts directly to Plex.
Run a TV-only post-bridge Plex/WebDAV verification gate before any user-visible TV surfacing.
Keep the 7 REVIEW pilot TV rows held and retryable for cache/source review.
Keep movie remediation separate because current full Materialized QA REVIEW is movie-only Plex DB visibility.
Keep generic/movie/unscoped expansion blocked while TV and movies cannot safely run concurrently.
Apply Plex Online Media Sources opt-out once a valid account session/token is available so Plex Live TV shows only curated Threadfin/DVR channels.
